$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `connection_profiles` repo (Phase-2),
the workhorse profile repo (~29 columns). One committed file drives BOTH
sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-connection-profiles-fixture.ts +
connection-profiles-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness connection_profiles tier-2 test).
What this repo adds over the prior ports: the widest marshaling surface yet —
three enum TEXT columns (provider, transport, pseudoToolMode), MANY boolean
columns (courierDeltaMode, isDefault, isCheap, allowWebSearch,
useNativeWebSearch, allowToolUse, isDangerousCompatible, supportsImageUpload),
two nullable REAL int-override columns (maxContext, maxTokens — REAL because
`.positive()` is a min only, no max), five REAL token counters (sortIndex,
totalTokens, totalPromptTokens, totalCompletionTokens, messageCount), three
nullable string columns (apiKeyId, baseUrl, modelClass), a JSON array column
(tags), and the open-JSON object column (parameters).
OPEN-JSON KEY-ORDER (seam — now CLOSED): `parameters` is arbitrary JSON. v4
serializes via JSON.stringify (INSERTION order); the Rust port uses serde_json
with the `preserve_order` feature (enabled workspace-wide), which ALSO
preserves insertion order. The SECOND create below uses a MULTI-KEY object with
keys in DELIBERATELY NON-SORTED order (zeta, alpha, topP) so sorted order
(alpha, topP, zeta) differs from insertion order — the green diff PROVES the
port emits insertion order, not sorted. Documented in the module.
No built-in guard (the ApiKey table + token-usage $inc helpers are out of
scope — only the main entity create/update/delete is ported).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders/tags/text-replacement-rules fixtures use. NOT the real Friday pepper;
safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.
Every column is supplied explicitly on create (no reliance on Zod defaults).

seed.$comment: Row A: nulls for apiKeyId/baseUrl/modelClass/maxContext/maxTokens; parameters {}; courier transport; varied booleans; zero counters.
seed.name: Manual Claude (Courier)

seed.$comment: Row B: all nullable fields set; integer-valued maxContext (128000) + maxTokens (8192); single-key parameters; api transport; non-zero token counters; the OTHER booleans flipped.
seed.name: OpenAI GPT (API)

ops.comment: New profile with nulls + parameters {}. Exercises null apiKeyId/baseUrl/modelClass/maxContext/maxTokens, courier transport, allowToolUse false, zero counters.

New profile with all fields set incl. integer-valued maxContext (200000) + a MULTI-KEY parameters object whose keys are in NON-SORTED order (zeta, alpha, topP -- sorted would be alpha, topP, zeta), proving the open-JSON column preserves v4's JSON.stringify INSERTION order (serde_json preserve_order). Exercises api transport, native pseudoToolMode, all booleans true, non-zero counters, a tag.
Update seed row B: rename, set maxTokens (integer-valued REAL), flip two booleans, bump a token counter. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row A: replace tags array + set sortIndex. Exercises the JSON array column + a REAL counter on update.
Delete the first created profile (Ollama Local).
